testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.title: Research: audit launch paths and pick CLI integration strategy
userStories.description:
## Goal

Before writing any code, decide **how** the CLI will invoke the same launcher logic the UI uses. Two plausible strategies:

**A. Extract the launcher core into a shared module**
- Move the meaty parts of `launchRalphForTicket` / `launchRalphForEpic` (src/api/ralph.ts) out of `createServerFn({...}).handler(...)` into pure functions in `core/` or a new `src/lib/ralph-launch/` module.
- Keep the server function as a thin wrapper.
- CLI imports the same pure function directly.
- Pros: no network dependency, works offline, faster, no CORS/auth concerns.
- Cons: requires decoupling filesystem/db access from TanStack Start runtime.

**B. Have the CLI call the running local server over HTTP**
- CLI POSTs to `localhost:4242/api/...` if the server is running; errors otherwise.
- Pros: guaranteed behavioral parity, no refactor.
- Cons: requires the dev server to be running; introduces HTTP failure modes; harder to script.

## Deliverables

1. A short design note committed to `plans/specs/{ticket-id}-cli-launch-parity.md` using the 6-layer spec template.
2. A recommendation (A or B or hybrid) with rationale covering: behavioral parity, testability, offline use, error surfaces, complexity.
3. A list of the exact functions/types that would move (or endpoints that would be called), file-by-file.
4. An inventory of every provider's launch code path (claude-code, vscode, cursor, cursor-agent, copilot-cli, codex, opencode) and the docker/sandbox branch — confirm none have UI-only dependencies (React hooks, toast calls, `useSettings`, etc.).

## Acceptance

- Spec file exists in `plans/specs/`
- Recommendation is explicit with numbered rationale
- Every provider path is traced and annotated
- Next tickets can be worked without ambiguity

## Notes

Per project memory: bake research into tickets for anything touching multiple external CLIs. Use subagents to read provider behavior if needed.

userStories.title: Extract Ralph launcher core into CLI-importable module
userStories.description:
## Goal

Based on the research ticket's recommendation (assume strategy A), refactor the launcher logic so both the TanStack Start server functions **and** the CLI can use it.

## Current shape (src/api/ralph.ts)

- `launchRalphForTicket = createServerFn({method:"POST"}).inputValidator(...).handler(async ({data}) => { ... })`
- `launchRalphForEpic` — same pattern
- Handlers do: settings lookup, ticket/project lookup, prompt profile generation, PRD write, context write, script generation, terminal/editor/docker launch dispatch, session creation, telemetry.

## Target shape

- `src/lib/ralph-launch/launch-ticket.ts` exports `launchRalphForTicket(db, input): Promise<Result>` — plain async function, no createServerFn.
- Same for `launch-epic.ts`.
- `src/api/ralph.ts` keeps the `createServerFn` wrappers but delegates to the new modules:
  ```ts
  export const launchRalphForTicket = createServerFn({method:"POST"})
    .inputValidator((data: LaunchTicketInput) => data)
    .handler(async ({data}) => launchRalphForTicketCore(db, data));
  ```
- All existing tests continue to pass (`src/api/ralph-launchers.test.ts`, `src/api/ralph-script-cursor-agent.test.ts`, etc.).

## Constraints

- No behavior change — same artifacts, same terminal command, same session creation, same telemetry.
- Pure logic must not import `@tanstack/react-start` or anything browser-only.
- DB access stays via the passed `db` handle (same as `core/`).
- Settings, Docker, terminal launchers should be callable without a running HTTP server.

## Acceptance

- New module exists, has the core functions, no TanStack imports
- Server functions are thin wrappers
- `pnpm check` passes
- All existing launch tests pass unchanged
- A smoke test confirms the core function can be called from a plain Node script (no Vite/Nitro runtime)

userStories.title: Add `brain-dump workflow launch-epic` and `launch-ticket` CLI commands
userStories.description:
## Goal

Ship the CLI commands that mimic the UI's Launch menu.

## New actions

In `cli/commands/workflow.ts`, add:

```
brain-dump workflow launch-epic \
  --epic <id> \
  [--provider claude-code|vscode|cursor|cursor-agent|copilot-cli|codex|opencode] \
  [--terminal <name>] \
  [--max-iterations <n>] \
  [--sandbox] \
  [--pretty]

brain-dump workflow launch-ticket \
  --ticket <id> \
  [--provider ...] \
  [--terminal ...] \
  [--max-iterations <n>] \
  [--sandbox] \
  [--pretty]
```

## Implementation

- Import the core functions from the module created in the refactor ticket.
- Translate `--provider` → `aiBackend` / `workingMethodOverride` exactly as the UI does (confirm in research ticket).
- Translate `--terminal` → `preferredTerminal`.
- Translate `--sandbox` → `useSandbox: true`.
- Use the project's existing `requireFlag` / `requireEnumFlag` / `numericFlag` / `boolFlag` helpers.
- Register both actions in `cli/lib/command-registry.ts` with descriptions and flag metadata so help output and completions work.
- Output the success/failure result via `outputResult(result, pretty)` so the existing JSON and `--pretty` modes both work.

## Error handling

Follow project rule "No Silent Failures":
- Provider not found on system → exit non-zero with an actionable message (`"cursor-agent not installed. Install via ... or use --provider claude-code"`).
- Ticket/epic not found → clear error.
- Missing required flag → `requireFlag` already throws a useful error.

## Acceptance

- `brain-dump workflow launch-epic --help` shows all flags with descriptions
- `brain-dump workflow launch-ticket --ticket <id> --provider copilot-cli` actually spawns Copilot CLI in a terminal with the right prompt
- Same for every supported provider (at least one smoke-test per provider on the dev machine)
- `brain-dump workflow launch-epic --epic <id>` works without `--provider` (falls back to project default, same as UI)
- JSON output on success contains the session id, provider used, and terminal command issued
- `pnpm check` passes

userStories.title: End-to-end verify CLI launch against all providers + update docs
acceptanceCriteria: claude-code — terminal opens with Claude in the project dir, PRD + context files exist, session row created
vscode — VS Code window opens with context file, session row created
cursor — Cursor window opens with context file
cursor-agent — terminal opens running the cursor-agent CLI
copilot-cli — terminal opens running `copilot` with the right prompt
codex — terminal opens with Codex CLI
`--sandbox` with claude-code spawns inside Docker (per existing UI behavior)
Epic launch (`launch-epic`) produces the same starts-implementation-workflow behavior as the UI (workflowState flipped, PRD built from epic tickets)
userStories.description:
## Goal

Close the loop: prove the new CLI commands produce the same effect as the UI's Launch menu across every provider, then document them.

## Verification checklist

For each backend, run `brain-dump workflow launch-ticket --ticket <test-ticket> --provider <name>` on a real machine, and confirm:

- [ ] claude-code — terminal opens with Claude in the project dir, PRD + context files exist, session row created
- [ ] vscode — VS Code window opens with context file, session row created
- [ ] cursor — Cursor window opens with context file
- [ ] cursor-agent — terminal opens running the cursor-agent CLI
- [ ] copilot-cli — terminal opens running `copilot` with the right prompt
- [ ] codex — terminal opens with Codex CLI
- [ ] opencode — terminal opens with opencode
- [ ] `--sandbox` with claude-code spawns inside Docker (per existing UI behavior)
- [ ] Epic launch (`launch-epic`) produces the same starts-implementation-workflow behavior as the UI (workflowState flipped, PRD built from epic tickets)

Per project memory ("Always Build End-to-End"): verify the FULL chain — CLI invocation → shared core → spawned terminal → session row in DB → visible in the UI's active sessions panel.

## Docs to update

- `CLAUDE.md` — add the two new actions to the CLI section
- `docs/cli.md` — full reference entries with flags, examples, error cases
- `README.md` (if it lists CLI examples) — add one example
- `docs/mcp-tools.md` is unaffected (MCP workflow tool is separate)

## Acceptance

- All provider rows in the checklist verified
- Docs updated and reviewed
- One integration test in `cli/__tests__/` that exercises `launch-ticket` against a fake backend (spy on the launcher core to confirm correct wiring — not a real spawn)
- `pnpm check` passes

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run `pnpm type-check` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm lint` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test` - all tests must pass
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicTitle: CLI Parity for Launch Epic/Ticket with Provider Choice
epicDescription:
## Why

Brain Dump's UI has a "Launch" menu that kicks off a Ralph loop for an epic or ticket with a chosen AI backend (claude-code, vscode, cursor, cursor-agent, copilot-cli, codex, opencode). This is implemented by `launchRalphForEpic` / `launchRalphForTicket` in `src/api/ralph.ts`, which dispatch through `src/api/ralph-launchers.ts` and `src/api/ralph-script.ts` to spawn the chosen provider in a terminal.

The CLI has **no equivalent**. `workflow start-epic` only flips DB state; `session create` only inserts a session row. There is no `--provider` flag anywhere, and no terminal spawning from any CLI command.

## Goal

Add `brain-dump workflow launch --epic <id>` (and `--ticket <id>`) with `--provider <name>`, `--terminal <name>`, `--max-iterations <n>`, `--sandbox` so any agent (Claude, me, Ralph itself, a remote trigger, a hotkey) can start an epic/ticket loop from a terminal with the provider of its choice — reaching full parity with the UI's Launch menu.

## Scope

- Extract the launcher logic from TanStack Start server functions into a shared module consumable by both the server and the CLI (CLI can't import `createServerFn` handlers directly).
- New CLI action(s): `workflow launch-epic` and `workflow launch-ticket` (or a single `launch` action with mutually-exclusive flags).
- Provider validation + discovery errors surface as clean CLI exit codes with actionable messages.
- Tests covering each provider path and flag validation.
- Update `docs/cli.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and examples.

## Non-goals

- No new UI changes — the UI already has Launch.
- No new providers — we're exposing existing backends through the CLI, not adding any.
- Docker sandbox support should work if it works in the UI, but no new sandbox capabilities.

## Why this matters

Unlocks scripting, remote triggers, hotkeys, and AI-driven autonomy — e.g. "start the auth epic with copilot-cli" from anywhere without opening the UI.
